visitorImpactNote: Hanifaru Bay (within Baa Atoll Biosphere Reserve) is the regional protection success story. Hanifaru Bay and a handful of designated MPAs are well-enforced; the wider Maldives still has resort-driven coastal impacts. Choose Green Fins operators on remote atolls.
Hanifaru Bay and a handful of designated MPAs are well-enforced; the wider Maldives still has resort-driven coastal impacts. Choose Green Fins operators on remote atolls.

One of the world's best-managed reef MPAs. Buy the Rp 1,000,000 conservation permit, dive with Green Fins–verified operators, and skip single-use plastics on the boat — your Rp 250,000 daily fee directly funds enforcement.
Komodo National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with strict no-take zoning. Permit fees fund rangers — choose operators who park on moorings rather than anchors.

Bali's reef diving sits outside strict MPA frameworks; village-level conservation initiatives at Tulamben are growing. Coral Triangle outside formal MPAs — local fishing communities depend on these reefs. Tip local guides directly; buy reef-safe sunscreen before leaving home.

Bunaken Marine National Park has been formally protected since 1991 with village-based ranger patrols. Local marine sanctuaries are well-run; the wider region still recovering from decades of destructive fishing. Choose dive shops that pay sanctuary fees directly to the village.

Monad Shoal is a Marine Protected Area with thresher-specific dive protocols. Local marine sanctuaries are well-run; the wider region still recovering from decades of destructive fishing. Choose dive shops that pay sanctuary fees directly to the village.
Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park is fully no-take and ranger-patrolled year-round. Liveaboards are the only access — book outfits that contribute to the conservation levy.

Apo Reef Natural Park has strict zoning and a daily diver cap. Local marine sanctuaries are well-run; the wider region still recovering from decades of destructive fishing. Choose dive shops that pay sanctuary fees directly to the village.

Local government MPA on Pescador; the wider coast is mixed. Mixed protection here. Choose Green Fins–verified operators and skip single-use plastics — Philippines coastal waste flow is among the highest in the world.
Similan / Surin national parks close seasonally to allow reef recovery. Liveaboards only. Choose operators that respect closures.
Koh Tao reefs are heavily loaded with training divers — picking ecology-focused dive schools helps reduce shallow-reef impact.
Similan / Surin national parks close seasonally to allow reef recovery. Liveaboards only. Choose operators that respect closures.

Sipadan is a strict no-take zone with a daily 178-permit cap. Apply months ahead. Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park is fully no-take and ranger-patrolled year-round. Liveaboards are the only access — book outfits that contribute to the conservation levy.

Mabul village fishery + dive resorts share the reef; community engagement programs are growing. Coral Triangle outside formal MPAs — local fishing communities depend on these reefs. Tip local guides directly; buy reef-safe sunscreen before leaving home.

Remote atoll with naval-base access constraints rather than formal MPA. Remote Red Sea zones (Sudan, Saudi, Eritrea) have less enforcement infrastructure. Picking liveaboards that participate in reef research helps fund data collection.

Myanmar reefs face limited monitoring and permit-heavy access. Remote Red Sea zones (Sudan, Saudi, Eritrea) have less enforcement infrastructure. Picking liveaboards that participate in reef research helps fund data collection.
Koh Rong Marine Fisheries Management Area established 2016. Koh Tao reefs are heavily loaded with training divers — picking ecology-focused dive schools helps reduce shallow-reef impact.
Hon Mun MPA was Vietnam's first MPA. Koh Tao reefs are heavily loaded with training divers — picking ecology-focused dive schools helps reduce shallow-reef impact.
Subtropical reefs with limited formal MPA coverage. Choose operators that pay into local fisher cooperatives.
Japanese reefs are at the cool edge of coral range. Most protection is via prefectural fishing co-op rules rather than formal MPAs.
Ogasawara Islands are UNESCO World Heritage. Japanese reefs are at the cool edge of coral range. Most protection is via prefectural fishing co-op rules rather than formal MPAs.
Subtropical reefs with limited formal MPA coverage. Choose operators that pay into local fisher cooperatives.
Subtropical reefs with limited formal MPA coverage. Choose operators that pay into local fisher cooperatives.

Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park covers Wandoor reefs. Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.

Lakshadweep is permit-only; foreign divers face significant access restrictions. Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.

Hikkaduwa Marine Sanctuary was Sri Lanka's first MPA. Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.
Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.

Aqaba Marine Park has been a no-fishing zone for divers since 1998. Ras Mohammed and surrounding marine parks are well-enforced; daily park fees fund rangers. The wider Red Sea sees heavy shipping and coastal building pressure.

Daymaniyat Islands Nature Reserve has whale-shark protection. Gulf of Oman and UAE reefs face thermal extremes and intense coastal building. Choose operators participating in reef-restoration nurseries.
Gulf of Oman and UAE reefs face thermal extremes and intense coastal building. Choose operators participating in reef-restoration nurseries.

Saudi Arabia's NEOM and Red Sea Project mega-developments are introducing new MPA frameworks. Remote Red Sea zones (Sudan, Saudi, Eritrea) have less enforcement infrastructure. Picking liveaboards that participate in reef research helps fund data collection.

Brothers/Daedalus/Elphinstone are part of the Marine Protected Area network. Ras Mohammed and surrounding marine parks are well-enforced; daily park fees fund rangers. The wider Red Sea sees heavy shipping and coastal building pressure.
Ras Mohammed and surrounding marine parks are well-enforced; daily park fees fund rangers. The wider Red Sea sees heavy shipping and coastal building pressure.

Sudan's Sanganeb and Dungonab MPAs are recent UNESCO listings; enforcement is modest. Remote Red Sea zones (Sudan, Saudi, Eritrea) have less enforcement infrastructure. Picking liveaboards that participate in reef research helps fund data collection.
Remote Red Sea zones (Sudan, Saudi, Eritrea) have less enforcement infrastructure. Picking liveaboards that participate in reef research helps fund data collection.
Whale-shark seasonal tourism in the Gulf of Tadjoura has limited formal regulation. Choose code-of-conduct-following operators.

Mnemba is a privately-managed reserve adjacent to Zanzibar. Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.

Watamu Marine National Park is one of Kenya's oldest MPAs. Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.
Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.
iSimangaliso Wetland Park (Sodwana, Cape Vidal) is UNESCO World Heritage. Aliwal Shoal is a protected MPA. Pay the day fee, follow shark-dive protocols.

iSimangaliso Wetland Park (UNESCO) covers Sodwana — one of the world's most thermally-buffered subtropical reefs. iSimangaliso Wetland Park (Sodwana, Cape Vidal) is UNESCO World Heritage. Aliwal Shoal is a protected MPA. Pay the day fee, follow shark-dive protocols.
Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.
Seychelles recently designated 30% of its EEZ as protected. Live with the conservation tag fees — they fund enforcement.
Seychelles recently designated 30% of its EEZ as protected. Live with the conservation tag fees — they fund enforcement.
Indian Ocean / East African coast has formal MPAs on paper but enforcement is patchy. Tip local guides directly; support community-conservancy diving where available.
Atlantic island reefs (Azores, Canaries, Cape Verde) are partially protected. Pelagic fishing pressure dominates; pick operators that support marine-science partnerships.
São Tomé reefs see modest dive-tourism pressure but significant industrial-fishing pressure on the EEZ. Operators are part-funded by visitor fees.

Cozumel Reefs National Park manages the western fringing reef. Dive-tag fee is mandatory. Mesoamerican Reef is partially protected by national parks. Sargassum and SCTLD are the dominant pressures. Support operators participating in coral nurseries.
Cenotes are freshwater cave systems on private + ejido land. Pay the entry fee; respect cave-diving training requirements.

topPressures: illegal industrial fishing on EEZ edges
visitorImpactNote: Revillagigedo National Park is a 148,000 km² no-take zone. Galápagos Marine Reserve recently expanded to 198,000 km². Industrial fishing on the EEZ edge remains a major issue — pick operators who back enforcement campaigns.

The Blue Hole sits within the Belize Barrier Reef Reserve, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Mesoamerican Reef is partially protected by national parks. Sargassum and SCTLD are the dominant pressures. Support operators participating in coral nurseries.

Turneffe Atoll Marine Reserve protects most of the atoll. Mesoamerican Reef is partially protected by national parks. Sargassum and SCTLD are the dominant pressures. Support operators participating in coral nurseries.

Bay Islands National Marine Park has paper protection; enforcement is patchy. Lower-protection Caribbean. The biggest pressures are SCTLD disease and overfishing — support operators that participate in coral-restoration nurseries.

Roatán Marine Park is operated by an NGO funded by diver day-fees. Lower-protection Caribbean. The biggest pressures are SCTLD disease and overfishing — support operators that participate in coral-restoration nurseries.
Cocos Island National Park is no-take; illegal longline fishing on EEZ edges is the persistent threat. Support operators that fund anti-poaching patrols.
Mesoamerican Reef is partially protected by national parks. Sargassum and SCTLD are the dominant pressures. Support operators participating in coral nurseries.
Coiba National Park is a former penal-colony-turned-park; protection is on paper, enforcement budget is thin. Park fees go directly to rangers.
Lower-protection Caribbean. The biggest pressures are SCTLD disease and overfishing — support operators that participate in coral-restoration nurseries.

topPressures: illegal industrial fishing on EEZ edges
visitorImpactNote: Malpelo Fauna and Flora Sanctuary is no-take; illegal fishing on EEZ edges remains the main threat. Galápagos Marine Reserve recently expanded to 198,000 km². Industrial fishing on the EEZ edge remains a major issue — pick operators who back enforcement campaigns.

Seaflower Biosphere Reserve protects the wider archipelago. Mesoamerican Reef is partially protected by national parks. Sargassum and SCTLD are the dominant pressures. Support operators participating in coral nurseries.

topPressures: illegal industrial fishing on EEZ edges
visitorImpactNote: Galápagos Marine Reserve recently expanded to 198,000 km². Industrial fishing on the EEZ edge remains a major issue — pick operators who back enforcement campaigns.

visitorImpactNote: Galápagos Marine Reserve recently expanded to 198,000 km². Industrial fishing on the EEZ edge remains a major issue — pick operators who back enforcement campaigns.
Fernando de Noronha is a strict national park with daily visitor caps. Environmental preservation fee is mandatory on entry; rules are tightly enforced.
Atlantic island reefs (Azores, Canaries, Cape Verde) are partially protected. Pelagic fishing pressure dominates; pick operators that support marine-science partnerships.

Los Roques National Park has been protected since 1972. Lower-protection Caribbean. The biggest pressures are SCTLD disease and overfishing — support operators that participate in coral-restoration nurseries.

Bonaire National Marine Park funds itself with the mandatory $40 STINAPA tag. Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.
Lower-protection Caribbean. The biggest pressures are SCTLD disease and overfishing — support operators that participate in coral-restoration nurseries.
Bahamas has banned commercial shark fishing nationwide since 2011 — a major win. Coastal-zone enforcement is patchier; cruise-port locations bear the brunt.

Exuma Cays Land and Sea Park is a 22-mile no-take zone — one of the world's first. Bahamas has banned commercial shark fishing nationwide since 2011 — a major win. Coastal-zone enforcement is patchier; cruise-port locations bear the brunt.

Cayman Islands Marine Parks are well-enforced; lionfish removal is encouraged. Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.
Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.

Saba Marine Park is fully no-take and tightly managed. Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.

STENAPA manages the Statia National Marine Park. Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.
Smaller Eastern Caribbean islands have modest MPA frameworks. Cruise-port locations bear concentrated impact.
Cenotes are freshwater cave systems on private + ejido land. Pay the entry fee; respect cave-diving training requirements.
Poor Knights Islands Marine Reserve is one of the world's first no-take MPAs — go with operators that respect the boundary.

GBR Marine Park is zoned no-take in Marine Park 'green zones'. The biggest pressure here is land-based runoff from Queensland farming — diving low-impact and supporting reef-restoration initiatives helps.

Ningaloo Marine Park covers the whole reef; whale-shark interactions are tightly regulated. GBR Marine Park is zoned no-take in Marine Park 'green zones'. The biggest pressure here is land-based runoff from Queensland farming — diving low-impact and supporting reef-restoration initiatives helps.
Cape Byron Marine Park covers Julian Rocks. Strict no-take zones around the rocks — boats anchor on designated moorings only.

Mahonia Na Dari (Walindi area) runs research and education partnerships locally. PNG reef country has the lowest dive-tourism footprint on Earth. Customary tenure means village fees go directly to landowners — pay them.
PNG reef country has the lowest dive-tourism footprint on Earth. Customary tenure means village fees go directly to landowners — pay them.

Shark Reef Marine Reserve is a community-run LMMA funded by dive operators. Fiji's locally-managed marine areas (LMMAs) are community-led conservation models — buy the village fee, respect taboo periods.
Fiji's locally-managed marine areas (LMMAs) are community-led conservation models — buy the village fee, respect taboo periods.
Palau National Marine Sanctuary protects 80% of the EEZ as no-take. The Palau Pristine Paradise Pledge is signed on arrival — read it and live it.
Palau National Marine Sanctuary protects 80% of the EEZ as no-take. The Palau Pristine Paradise Pledge is signed on arrival — read it and live it.
Yap reefs are protected under traditional landowner control. Yap, Chuuk, and FSM mix traditional tenure with national park designations. Chuuk wrecks are legally protected as cultural heritage.

Chuuk wrecks are legally protected as cultural heritage under Micronesian law. Yap, Chuuk, and FSM mix traditional tenure with national park designations. Chuuk wrecks are legally protected as cultural heritage.
Most French Polynesian atolls have community-managed protection. Choose operators that funnel fees to the local commune.
UNESCO Man and the Biosphere reserve with strict fishing rules. Diving load is low — choose operators that maintain the historical shark-friendly handling protocols at the South Pass.
Solomon Islands reefs are customary-owned; conservation works through village protocols. Tip directly to communities; respect WWII wreck etiquette.
Niue declared 40% of its EEZ as the Moana Mahu Marine Park in 2022. Minimal tourism pressure — diving here is unusually low-impact.
Mediterranean MPAs (Medes Islands, Cabrera, etc.) are recovering from decades of trawling. Cited dive sites are healthier inside MPA boundaries — pay any access fees.

Mar de las Calmas Marine Reserve protects El Hierro's diving area. Atlantic island reefs (Azores, Canaries, Cape Verde) are partially protected. Pelagic fishing pressure dominates; pick operators that support marine-science partnerships.
Mediterranean coastal diving outside MPAs. The reef ecology here is sponge / gorgonian / algae — primary pressures are intense fishing and marine traffic.
Mediterranean coastal diving outside MPAs. The reef ecology here is sponge / gorgonian / algae — primary pressures are intense fishing and marine traffic.
Atlantic island reefs (Azores, Canaries, Cape Verde) are partially protected. Pelagic fishing pressure dominates; pick operators that support marine-science partnerships.
Mediterranean coastal diving outside MPAs. The reef ecology here is sponge / gorgonian / algae — primary pressures are intense fishing and marine traffic.

Kornati National Park covers most of the archipelago. Mediterranean MPAs (Medes Islands, Cabrera, etc.) are recovering from decades of trawling. Cited dive sites are healthier inside MPA boundaries — pay any access fees.
Florida Keys National Marine Sanctuary protects the reef but can't protect the water flowing from the Everglades. Choose restoration-affiliated operators and pay the sanctuary fee.
West Hawaii reefs are partially protected. Pick reef-safe sunscreen (banned in Hawaii since 2021) and follow operator briefings on minimum distances from cleaning manta rays.
Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary is well-managed; kelp loss is the primary pressure, driven by warming + urchin booms.

Jardines de la Reina is the Caribbean's largest no-take reserve; permit-only access. Caribbean MPAs (Cayman, Saba, Bonaire, Bonaire, Cuba JdR) are some of the world's best-managed. Pay the conservation tag fee at entry and join a lionfish cull if offered.
Smaller Eastern Caribbean islands have modest MPA frameworks. Cruise-port locations bear concentrated impact.
Smaller Eastern Caribbean islands have modest MPA frameworks. Cruise-port locations bear concentrated impact.
Mediterranean coastal diving outside MPAs. The reef ecology here is sponge / gorgonian / algae — primary pressures are intense fishing and marine traffic.
Smaller Eastern Caribbean islands have modest MPA frameworks. Cruise-port locations bear concentrated impact.

Osprey Reef sits in the Coral Sea Marine Park — Australia's largest reserve, established 2018. GBR Marine Park is zoned no-take in Marine Park 'green zones'. The biggest pressure here is land-based runoff from Queensland farming — diving low-impact and supporting reef-restoration initiatives helps.

Tela Marine Research Center limits dive groups to 30 to 40 divers per day. AMATELA Marine Wildlife Refuge (86,259 ha, declared 2018) and Jeannette Kawas National Park (Punta Sal, 1994) provide formal protection with active enforcement. Closed fishing seasons under AMATELA produced roughly a 500 percent fish biomass increase in coastal lagoons — choose operators that work directly with PROLANSATE foundation.
